text: Houthi ballistic missile targeting Tel Aviv area intercepted. Separately, 50+ Hezbollah rockets fired at northern Israel — 2 lightly wounded. Triple-axis coordination continues: Iran + Hezbollah + Houthis all striking in the same evening window.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog confirmed all three attack vectors.

text: Markets staged remarkable intraday reversal: Dow was -600 pts but closed just -61 (-0.13%). S&P actually closed +0.11%. Recovery driven by Iran-Oman Hormuz navigation regime draft news — first diplomatic framework for post-war transit. Oil still at $109/$111.
verificationNote: CNBC and Motley Fool confirmed market close data and recovery narrative.

text: Day 34 EVE (3:58 PM PDT): Army Chief fired; Petah Tikva cluster strike (girl critical); Houthi + Hezbollah evening attacks; Macron 'unrealistic' Hormuz military; markets recovered (S&P +0.11%); Brent $109.35/WTI $111; gas $4.08; 40-nation Hormuz summit; April 6 deadline in 4 days.
verificationNote: Compiled from Breaking Defense, Times of Israel, Bloomberg, CNBC, AAA.

text: Rubio on Hannity: 'We can see the finish line. It's not today, it's not tomorrow, but it is coming.' Confirms US-Iran message exchanges via Pakistan with potential for direct meeting. Also signals US will reexamine NATO relations post-war. Significant diplomatic shift.
verificationNote: Breitbart and GMA News confirmed Rubio quotes from Hannity interview.

text: GCC just backed a draft UN Security Council resolution authorizing 'all defensive means necessary' to reopen Hormuz. Bahrain (current UNSC president) proposed it. Russia and China expected to veto. Vote scheduled for FRIDAY April 4. Diplomatic pressure intensifying ahead of Trump's April 6 deadline.
verificationNote: Times of Israel and The National confirmed resolution details.

text: Physical Brent crude just hit $141.36/barrel — highest since 2008 financial crisis. WTI settled at $113.03 after 12.9% single-session surge, biggest daily gain since war began. IEA warns April will be 'much worse than March.' Second strategic reserves release under consideration.
verificationNote: CNBC confirmed Dated Brent $141.36 and WTI $113.03 settlement.

text: IDF 162nd Division killed 40 Hezbollah fighters in new push deeper into southern Lebanon — reached new section of Litani River. Lebanese Army withdrawing from border villages. IDF ordering demolition of homes and bridges south of Litani. 7 civilians killed in related strikes.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera and Jerusalem Post confirmed IDF operations.

text: IRGC struck Amazon AWS in Bahrain for the FOURTH time since war began. Part of broader campaign against 18 named US tech companies. Meanwhile US intelligence assesses ~50% of Iran's missile launchers still intact and thousands of one-way attack drones remain.
verificationNote: Anadolu Agency confirmed Amazon strike. Haaretz confirmed US intel assessment.

text: Trump Truth Social just now: 'US military hasn't even started destroying what's left in Iran. Bridges next, then Electric Power Plants!' April 6 Hormuz deadline now just 3 days away. Power grid strikes were postponed to April 6 amid talks — now being openly threatened again.
verificationNote: Al-Monitor and Gulf News confirmed Trump's Truth Social post.

text: Day 35 AM (11:12 PM PDT): Brent $109.35/WTI $113.03 (physical Brent $141.36!); gas $4.08; Trump threatens bridges + power plants; GCC backs UN Hormuz force vote Fri; Rubio 'finish line'; IDF kills 40 Hezbollah; IRGC hits Amazon 4th time; April 6 in 3 DAYS.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, Al-Monitor, Gulf News, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Breitbart.

text: Hezbollah massive Passover barrage: ~130 rockets at northern Israel in rapid succession. 30+ in a single salvo targeting Kiryat Ata/Haifa area. 4 lightly injured. Largest single barrage this week. Coordinated with Iranian cluster munition strikes.
verificationNote: Times of Israel liveblog confirmed rocket count and casualties.

text: Iran using cluster munition warheads on ballistic missiles targeting Kiryat Ata. 1 wounded. IDF warns this represents escalation in Iranian weapon types. Cluster munitions banned under Convention on Cluster Munitions (neither Iran nor Israel are signatories).
verificationNote: Haaretz confirmed cluster munition warheads and IDF assessment.

text: Kuwait hit twice today: drones struck Mina Al-Ahmadi refinery (3rd time, 466K bpd capacity) and missile hit desalination plant. Kuwait blames Iran, IRGC denies. Gulf civilian infrastructure increasingly targeted. Oil markets watching closely.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera and The National confirmed both Kuwait strikes.

text: IRGC claims US F-15E shot down near Qeshm Island in Hormuz. Debris photos surfacing online — clearly F-15E wreckage, NOT F-35 as initially claimed. CENTCOM denies: 'All aircraft safe and accounted for.' Second unconfirmed claim of F-35 over central Iran.
verificationNote: CENTCOM denies shootdown. The Aviationist analyzed debris as F-15E.

text: Hegseth fires Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George + 2 other generals DURING WARTIME. Unprecedented. Gen. LaNeve (Hegseth's former aide) becomes acting chief. Meanwhile Rubio-Hegseth feuding over ground troops. Command instability during active combat operations.
verificationNote: CBS News, Washington Post, CNN all confirmed firings.

text: Day 35 midday (5:50 AM PDT): Brent $109.03/WTI $111.54 (WTI>Brent inversion!); gas $4.09; 130 Hezbollah rockets; Iran cluster bombs; Kuwait refinery+desal hit; Iran claims F-15E shootdown (denied); Hegseth fires Army chief; Trump 'do what I tell them'; April 6 in 3 DAYS.
verificationNote: Compiled from CNBC, Haaretz, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, CBS News, The Aviationist.

text: Debris photos from Iran confirm downed aircraft is F-15E Strike Eagle from 494th Fighter Squadron, RAF Lakenheath (USAFE tail markings). Iran misidentified as F-35. One crew rescued by US SOF inside Iran. Second crew member search ongoing.
verificationNote: The Aviationist analysis confirmed by NBC News, Axios, CNN.

text: B1 Bridge between Tehran and Karaj — tallest bridge in Middle East — confirmed collapsed after two-wave US precision strike. i24News reports purpose was severing drone/missile supply routes to Iranian launch units targeting US/Israeli forces.
verificationNote: The War Zone, i24News, NPR, Fox News all confirmed.

text: IDF confirms elimination of Makram Atimi, commander of central Iranian ballistic missile unit, in Kermanshah airstrike. Atimi directed dozens of launches at Israel. Several battalion commanders also killed in same strike.
verificationNote: Jerusalem Post and Xinhua confirmed.

text: Abu Dhabi halts operations at Habshan gas facility (ADNOC's main gas complex) after interceptor debris caused fire. UAE engaged 18 ballistic missiles, 4 cruise missiles, 47 drones from Iran today. 12 injured in Ajban area.
verificationNote: Bloomberg and The National confirmed.

text: CNN reporting Iran retains half of its missile launchers and massive drone fleet despite 35 days of strikes. IDF struck 50 ballistic missile sites during Passover but Iran's capacity remains significant. Cluster munitions now routine — Petah Tikva cratered near school.
verificationNote: CNN via Haaretz liveblog confirmed remaining Iranian capabilities.

text: UNSC vote on GCC Hormuz resolution officially delayed from Friday to Saturday April 5 (UN holiday). China opposes force authorization — resolution stripped of Chapter VII enforcement language. UK convenes 40+ countries separately on Hormuz.
verificationNote: Al-Monitor and NPR confirmed delays and coalition talks.

text: 100+ international law scholars publish open letter: US strikes on Iran may constitute war crimes. Cite legality of war decision, conduct of hostilities, Hegseth's dismantling of civilian protection structures, and Minab school strike (175+ killed).
verificationNote: Just Security published original letter. Al Jazeera and Common Dreams confirmed.

text: Day 35 afternoon (11:35 AM PDT): F-15E confirmed downed; B1 Bridge collapsed (8 dead, 95 wounded); IDF kills Atimi; 50 missile sites hit; Habshan halted; Batelco/AWS hit; Zarif peace plan; $1.5T budget; 100+ scholars war crimes letter; April 6 in 3 DAYS.
verificationNote: Compiled from NBC, NPR, JPost, Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, WaPo, Just Security.

text: BREAKING: Pentagon confirms F-15E Strike Eagle downed over central Iran — first manned US aircraft combat loss since Operation Epic Fury began. One crew member rescued by HC-130/HH-60G SAR package. Second crew member still missing — Iran reportedly offering bounty for live capture.
verificationNote: Confirmed by Military Times, CBS News, and The War Zone. IRGC released debris photos.

text: Iran expanding target set to Gulf civilian infrastructure: Kuwait Mina al-Ahmadi refinery on fire after drone strike (450K bpd capacity), Kuwait desalination plant hit (2nd in 4 days — 90% of drinking water at risk), UAE Habshan gas facility halted from debris. Saudi intercepted ~12 drones.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera, Khaleej Times, The National confirmed all strikes.

text: Hegseth fires Army Chief of Staff Gen. Randy George + 2 other generals (Hodne, Green) during active wartime operations. Gen. Christopher LaNeve named acting chief. Unprecedented wartime military leadership shakeup — reportedly over blocked diversity promotions.
verificationNote: Axios, CNN, Al Jazeera confirmed firings.

text: IDF confirmed 70+ strikes in Iran in past 24h. Key hit: IRGC ballistic missile commander Makram Atimi eliminated in Kermanshah airstrike. Central IRGC headquarters in Tehran also struck. Netanyahu claims 70% of Iran's steel production capacity now destroyed.
verificationNote: Jerusalem Post and Times of Israel confirmed Atimi elimination and steel claims.

text: Day 35 PM summary: Iran missile barrage on Israel (~10 BMs, mostly cluster munitions per IDF radio). Tel Aviv train station damaged by shrapnel. Petah Tikva large crater near school. Good Friday — markets closed. WTI-Brent inversion continues ($111.54 vs $109.03). April 6 Trump Hormuz deadline in 3 days.
